skill.name: writing-clearly-and-concisely
skill.description: Improve human-facing prose with clear writing rules, concise edits, and checks for common AI writing patterns.
skill.summary: Edit documentation, UI text, messages, and explanations for clarity and concision.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found Markdown prose, citations, image links, file-name references, and fenced examples. I found no executable code, prompt injection attempt, credential access, automatic network call, or automatic filesystem operation; the remaining risk is limited to external reference links and a documented manual install command.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Markdown Links
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings point to citations, image references, and source links in Markdown. The files contain no code that fetches these URLs or sends data to them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown links, footnotes, or image references. No network API, request library, or automatic upload behavior appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code spans and fenced examples. They name reference files or show text artifacts, not executable shell operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious characters are used for Markdown formatting. They are not Ruby syntax and are not interpreted by the skill runtime as command execution.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Manual Install Command
low_findings.description: The hidden home directory and filesystem findings come from a README installation example that copies the skill into a Claude skills folder. It is documentation for a user-run command, not code that runs automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is inside a fenced bash block under an Installation heading. It writes only to the expected local Claude skills directory and is not invoked by SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Prose Matched Security Terms
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography, reconnaissance, hard link, screen capture, C2 keyword, and IP findings resolve to ordinary prose, grammar examples, citations, or AI-writing examples. I found no malicious intent or executable behavior at the reviewed locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is a writing reference, not source code. The flagged terms are examples and quotations used to teach prose style or describe AI-writing artifacts.

content.user_title: Improve Prose for Clear Human Reading
content.value_statement: Dense or generic prose slows readers and weakens trust. This skill edits human-facing text with clear rules, concise wording, and checks for common AI patterns.
actual_capabilities: Rewrites documentation, README text, reports, and explanations for clearer prose.
Shortens verbose sentences while preserving the main meaning.
Applies active voice, positive phrasing, concrete language, and paragraph focus.
Flags common AI writing habits such as puffery, empty modifiers, and overused terms.
Guides focused edits by loading only the relevant reference section when needed.
Improves commit messages, pull request text, error messages, and UI copy.
limitations: It does not fact-check claims or verify sources by itself.
It is a writing guide, not a grammar checker with formal language parsing.
Some source examples use older style guidance that may need modern judgment.
It cannot guarantee that edited text will pass AI detection tools.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Technical Documentation
use_cases.description: Turn long setup notes or feature explanations into direct text that readers can scan and follow.

use_cases.title: Tighten Product and Interface Copy
use_cases.description: Replace vague claims with specific labels, errors, tooltips, and help text that explain what users need.

use_cases.title: Review AI-Drafted Prose
use_cases.description: Find inflated language, repetitive phrasing, and generic claims before publishing AI-assisted drafts.

prompt_templates.title: Make This Clearer
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner edit of a short paragraph.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the writing-clearly-and-concisely skill to edit this paragraph for clarity and concision. Keep the meaning unchanged: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Improve Developer Text
prompt_templates.scenario: Edit documentation, commit messages, or pull request text.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to revise this developer-facing text. Prefer active voice, concrete wording, and short sentences: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Remove AI-Sounding Language
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to identify AI writing patterns in this draft, then provide a cleaner revision with specific wording: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Apply Focused Style Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced edit with explanation of changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the relevant Elements of Style reference from the skill to revise this text. Explain the three most important edits after the revision: [paste text]

output_examples.input: This feature leverages cutting-edge technology to provide a seamless and impactful user experience.
output_examples.output: This feature updates the dashboard in real time.

output_examples.input: It is important to note that the API might potentially return an error in certain situations.
output_examples.output: The API returns an error when the token expires.

output_examples.input: The configuration file is read by the application during startup.
output_examples.output: The application reads the configuration file during startup.

best_practices: Paste the draft and state the audience before asking for edits.
Ask for a revision first, then request a short explanation of key changes.
Use the AI-pattern review before publishing text drafted by a model.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to verify facts without providing trusted sources.
Do not preserve promotional language when the audience needs plain information.
Do not load every reference file when one focused section answers the task.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps edit prose for clarity, brevity, active voice, and concrete language.

faq.question: Can it write new documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft and revise documentation when you provide the topic, audience, and details.

faq.question: Does it only follow The Elements of Style?
faq.answer: No. It also includes guidance for avoiding common AI writing patterns.

faq.question: Can it detect all AI-written text?
faq.answer: No. It can flag common patterns, but it cannot prove that text was written by AI.

faq.question: Is it useful for code comments?
faq.answer: Yes. It can make comments and docstrings shorter, clearer, and more specific.

faq.question: Does it run commands or access the network?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill content is Markdown guidance and reference material.
